note: BRAIN layer — value delivery queue for INTERESTED leads. HANDS layer executes: runs audits, prepares proposals, sends reply emails. Priority order listed below.

items.new_pain_point_discovered: No online booking system — self-reported lost customers
items.reply_summary: Confirmed Tuesday 2pm call. Identified booking gap independently.

audit_tasks.notes: Identify 2 free/low-cost booking options suitable for a small hair salon (e.g. Booksy, Fresha, Calendly). Include in proposal.

option_b.name: Full Fix
option_b.scope: SSL + mobile + booking widget integration + above-fold CTA

proposal_outline.notes: Claire mentioned the booking gap herself — Option B is the likely conversion. Do not anchor low. Present both, let her choose.

confirmation_email_template.subject: Re: Tuesday 2pm — looking forward to it
confirmation_email_template.body:
Hi Claire,

Tuesday 2pm works perfectly — I'll send over a calendar invite.

I've had a look at the site ahead of the call and put together a short audit with a few things worth talking through. I'll go over it with you on Tuesday, but the quick summary is:

- SSL fix: straightforward, takes an afternoon
- Mobile: needs a small tweak, not a rebuild
- Booking: this is probably the biggest opportunity — I've looked at two free options that would work well for a salon your size

See you Tuesday.

[Your name]
confirmation_email_template.notes: Send today to confirm the slot and tease the audit. Do not attach PDF yet — save it for the call.

items.reply_summary: Asking whether SSL/mobile can be bundled into full redesign — showing upsell appetite.

option_b.name: Full Redesign Bundle
option_b.scope: New design + SSL + mobile + contact form + portfolio gallery
option_b.notes: Better value per item. This is what he's hinting at.

option_c.name: Redesign + Ongoing SEO
option_c.scope: Option B + monthly local SEO to rank for 'print shop Sowerby Bridge'

reply_template.subject: Re: bundling it all together — yes, that's the better approach
reply_template.body:
Hi Rob,

Yes absolutely — bundling makes much more sense than doing the SSL as a standalone job.

Here's how I'd approach it:

**Option 1 — Full redesign bundle: £550**
New design + SSL + mobile + contact form + updated portfolio section. One job, one invoice. Usually 7-10 days.

**Option 2 — Redesign + monthly SEO: £600 + £75/month**
Everything in Option 1, plus I handle your local SEO ongoing — gets you ranking for 'print shop Sowerby Bridge' and nearby towns.

Given your 1-month timeframe, Option 1 is the easiest place to start. Happy to get going quickly if you want to confirm.

Shall I send a one-page scope document?

[Your name]
reply_template.notes: Rob is warm and on a 1-month horizon. Be direct on scope and price — don't hedge. Offer the scope doc as a natural next step (moves him forward without pressure).

items.new_pain_point_discovered: No online quote form — customers told to ring instead
items.reply_summary: Acknowledged the quote gap, said he's been meaning to sort it. Asked for pricing.

audit_tasks.notes: Identify 1-2 examples of similar local carpentry business sites with good quote request forms. Screenshot or link.

reply_template.subject: Re: Quick question about Ripon Road Carpentry
reply_template.body:
Tom,

Glad to hear it — that's exactly the kind of thing I help with.

For adding a quote form to an existing site, I typically charge £100-150 — it's a half-day job. The form captures: job type, location, rough timescale, and contact details. I also hook it up so enquiries land straight in your inbox (or a spreadsheet if you prefer).

I'll send over a couple of examples from similar carpentry businesses so you can see what it looks like in practice.

Would Tuesday or Wednesday suit for a quick 15-min call to confirm the spec?

[Your name]
reply_template.notes: He asked for pricing — give a number. Don't hedge. £100-150 is realistic and low-friction for a one-person trades business. Offering examples builds trust before the call.

summary.gdpr_note: All three leads replied voluntarily — legitimate interest basis confirmed. Log replies and actions in audit_log.
